snippets.title: Manual metric log with split-prefixed names
snippets.code:
metrics_log = []

for epoch in range(1, 11):
    model.fit(X_train, y_train)
    train_loss = model.evaluate(X_train, y_train, verbose=0)
    val_loss = model.evaluate(X_val, y_val, verbose=0)
    metrics_log.append({
        "epoch": epoch,
        "train/loss": float(train_loss),
        "val/loss": float(val_loss),
    })

print(metrics_log[-1])
snippets.explanation: Each epoch appends a dict with consistent split-prefixed keys. The same names can be pushed to MLflow, W&B, or a CSV without renaming.

snippets.title: sklearn: log train and val F1 per hyperparameter trial
snippets.code:
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

results = []
for n_estimators in [50, 100, 200]:
    clf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=n_estimators, random_state=0)
    clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
    row = {
        "n_estimators": n_estimators,
        "train/f1": f1_score(y_train, clf.predict(X_train)),
        "val/f1": f1_score(y_val, clf.predict(X_val)),
    }
    results.append(row)

best = max(results, key=lambda r: r["val/f1"])
print(best)
snippets.explanation: Model selection uses val/f1 only. train/f1 is logged for diagnostics but never ranks trials.

snippets.title: Log test metric once after selection
snippets.code:
final_clf = RandomForestClassifier(
    n_estimators=best["n_estimators"], random_state=0
)
final_clf.fit(X_train, y_train)

test_f1 = f1_score(y_test, final_clf.predict(X_test))
final_metrics = {"test/f1": test_f1, "phase": "final_report"}
print(final_metrics)
snippets.explanation: test/f1 is computed and logged once after the validation winner is chosen— not inside the per-epoch training loop.

snippets.title: PyTorch-style step and epoch scalars
snippets.explanation: Train loss logs frequently by step; validation loss logs once per epoch on the same step axis so curves share a comparable x-axis.
